analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every cuDNN convolution op is preceded by TWO convertTensor_kernel<float,float,float,(cudnnKernelDataType_t)2> layout-conversion kernels (NCHW->NHWC and back). The model runs in default contiguous NCHW, but cuDNN's TF32 tensor-op fprop path (cutlass_tensorop_s1688fprop / sm80_xmma ... nhwc) requires NHWC, so cuDNN inserts a permute before and after every conv. These convert kernels do zero math and exist purely to repack memory. The leading triton_poi_fused_convolution_0/1 op (7488 ns) is the same NCHW->NHWC repack on the network input.

transformation.description: Annotate the conv inputs and weights as torch.channels_last so the activations are already NHWC when they reach cuDNN. cuDNN then consumes them directly on its native NHWC tensor-op path and emits NHWC output, eliminating all 12 convertTensor permute kernels and the input-prep triton repack. No numerical change; pure overhead removal. Implemented as an FX/Inductor layout pass that emits aten.contiguous(memory_format=channels_last) on the 4D conv activations and pre-converts conv weight placeholders to channels_last once at compile time.
transformation.location: Custom backend layout pass applied to all aten.convolution.default nodes whose input is a 4D NCHW tensor.
fx_steps: node.update_arg(0, act_cl)  # weight pre-converted to channels_last as a folded constant
transformation.code_hint: Equivalent module-level form: model = model.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last); x = x.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last). In a backend pass, emit aten.contiguous(..., memory_format=channels_last) on conv activations so cuDNN sees NHWC and drops convertTensor.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes 12 full-tensor NCHW<->NHWC repacks (each reads+writes the whole activation) plus the 4-kernel input-prep triton repack.

optimizations.notes: Pure layout change, numerically exact. Highest-certainty win because the convert kernels are unambiguously zero-math overhead visible in every conv op. Synergizes with OPT-2: NHWC is also the preferred layout for bf16 tensor ops.

bottleneck.description: The dominant cost is the compute-bound conv GEMMs. The stage2/stage3 cutlass_tensorop_s1688fprop_optimized_tf32 kernels run ~91 us each and are genuinely compute-bound: sm_throughput 56-64%, tensor-core active 66-73%, DRAM throughput only 6-28%. They execute the TF32 tensor-op path. TF32 keeps the FP32 byte width but uses the half-rate-equivalent tensor op; switching to bf16 halves the tensor-op issue cycles and halves the activation/weight bytes staged through L2/SMEM. The four large conv GEMMs alone account for ~74% of attributed time.

transformation.description: Promote conv (and the linear head) compute to bf16: insert a cast of activation and weight to torch.bfloat16 in front of each aten.convolution.default / aten.addmm.default, keeping fp32 accumulate. cuDNN routes bf16 NHWC to the half-rate tensor-op path (s16816-class), roughly halving tensor-op cycles and halving the bytes moved by the DRAM-bound BN/ReLU triton kernels downstream (they then read/write half-width activations). Eval-mode inference tolerates bf16 well for this 3-stage CNN.
transformation.location: Custom backend dtype pass over all aten.convolution.default and aten.addmm.default nodes.
transformation.code_hint: Inductor folds redundant adjacent casts; downstream BN/ReLU triton fuses the back-cast into its existing epilogue. Prefer torch.autocast(dtype=bf16) semantics so accumulation stays fp32.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Halves activation/weight byte width: directly relieves the DRAM-bound BN/ReLU triton kernels (currently 80-89% DRAM) and halves tensor-op staging traffic for the GEMMs.

optimizations.notes: Largest absolute lever but changes numerics, hence medium confidence. Must run before OPT-3: per the register_buffer-after-dtype rule, the folded conv weight produced by Conv-BN folding must be created at the bf16 runtime dtype. Pairs with OPT-1 (NHWC is the preferred bf16 conv layout).

bottleneck.description: In eval mode BatchNorm is a fixed per-channel affine transform (running_mean, running_var, weight, bias are constants). Inductor lowered it to standalone triton kernels (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_norm_legit_no_training_relu_*) that re-read the conv output from DRAM, recompute (x-mean)*rsqrt(var+eps)*gamma+beta, apply ReLU, and write back. These kernels are strongly DRAM-bound (dram_throughput 85.96 / 89.55 / 85.78 / 83.03% on the dominant launches), with sm_throughput only 20-31% and zero tensor-core use - pure memory-bound elementwise passes that also fetch the four BN parameter tensors every launch.

transformation.description: Conv-BN folding for eval mode: collapse each _native_batch_norm_legit_no_training into the preceding convolution by rescaling the conv weight (w' = w * gamma / sqrt(var+eps)) and synthesizing a conv bias (b' = beta - gamma*mean/sqrt(var+eps)). BN becomes identity and is deleted from the graph; the only surviving elementwise is ReLU, which Inductor already fuses as the conv epilogue. This removes the per-element BN arithmetic and the four BN-parameter reads per kernel and lets the conv emit bias+ReLU directly. The folded weight is registered as a new constant buffer.
transformation.location: Custom backend pass matching (aten.convolution.default -> aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default) chains; valid only because is_training=False (no_training overload).
fx_steps: if conv.target is not torch.ops.aten.convolution.default or len(conv.users) != 1: continue
w, gamma, beta, mean, var = conv.args[1], bn.args[1], bn.args[2], bn.args[3], bn.args[4]
folded_w = gm.graph.call_function(_fold_weight, (w, gamma, var, eps))  # w*gamma/sqrt(var+eps)
folded_b = gm.graph.call_function(_fold_bias, (beta, gamma, mean, var, eps))  # beta-mean*gamma/sqrt(var+eps)
transformation.code_hint: torch.nn.utils.fuse_conv_bn_eval gives the closed-form fold. Compute folded constants once at compile and register_buffer them; ReLU stays as the conv epilogue Inductor already produces.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates the four BN parameter reads per launch and the BN normalization math; activation read/write (needed by ReLU) remains, so this trims the compute and parameter-fetch portion of the DRAM-bound BN/ReLU kernels rather than the full activation pass.

optimizations.notes: Mathematically exact in eval mode (no_training overload guarantees fixed stats). Must run AFTER OPT-2 (bf16): the register_buffer-after-dtype-promotion rule requires the folded weight/bias buffers allocated at the bf16 runtime dtype. The activation read/write floor bounds the impact; the convs (OPT-1, OPT-2) dominate the budget.

global_notes: 2 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 1.68% of attributed time): aten::addmm op_id=21 (0.96%) and op_id=42 (0.72%), the Linear classifier head (gemmSN_TN small-N cuBLAS path, 1 kernel each).

fused_kernel_double_count flag set: most cudnn_convolution operators and the BN operator carry is_fused==true. Durations are summed over the kernels listed under each operator; the convertTensor permute kernels are attributed inside their parent conv op, so they are counted within OPT-1's conv-op budget, not double-counted.

Profile spans 2 measured iterations (op_id sets 5-21 and 26-42 repeat the same forward). No capture_metadata.measure_iters field is present, so warm_up_inflation could not be evaluated by the multiple-of-iters test; iteration-2 kernel durations closely match iteration-1 (e.g. cutlass conv 91808 vs 91456 ns), so no dynamic_shapes variance.
duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection overhead and are used only for relative comparison within this profile, never as absolute latency.

knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present on disk and no MCP tools (context7, exa-search) were available; per documented fallback, Blackwell limits and FX API patterns were taken from model knowledge and novel transforms capped at medium confidence. Assumption: RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (GB202) ~188 SMs; warp_cycles_per_instruction is unavailable on Blackwell (all null in profile), so eligible_cycles_pct<20 was the latency-bound indicator.

Dependency DAG: OPT-2 (bf16, aten) -> OPT-3 (Conv-BN fold, aten) via the register_buffer-after-dtype-promotion rule; OPT-1 (channels_last, aten) is independent. All three are aten-level and the backend funnel applies them in listed order. No cycles.

ReLU is already fused by Inductor into the BN/conv triton epilogues (kernel names contain _relu_), so no separate ReLU-fusion pass is proposed; the remaining elementwise opportunity is exactly the Conv-BN fold in OPT-3.

Occupancy on the cutlass conv kernels is low (~8.3%) but expected for 150-register tensor-op kernels; they are compute/tensor-core bound, not occupancy-limited, so the lever is precision (OPT-2) not launch geometry.
